message:
fix(scheduler): skip stale entries in all pop methods to prevent dispatching cancelled tasks

All scheduler pop methods (pop_cancel_with_rng, pop_timed_only_with_hint,
pop_ready_only, pop_ready_only_with_hint, pop_any_lane_with_hint) now
loop until they find an entry whose task is still in the scheduled set,
instead of unconditionally returning the first popped entry.

Previously, a task that was cancelled and removed from the scheduled set
between being enqueued and being popped would still be returned to the
dispatcher. The dispatcher would then attempt to run a task that no
longer exists in the runtime's task table, causing either a panic or
silent corruption depending on the task storage implementation.

The fix changes each `if let Some(entry)` to `while let Some(entry)`
(or wraps the body in a `loop`), checking `self.scheduled.remove()`
as a filter. Entries that fail the remove check are effectively
discarded — they represent tasks that were cancelled after being
scheduled but before being dispatched. The timed lane variant
additionally breaks out of the loop when peeling reveals a
not-yet-due deadline, preserving the earliest-deadline-first invariant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
